record.field: Apache Airflow is an open-source platform to programmatically author, schedule and monitor data pipelines and workflows defined as Python code.

Apache Airflow is an open-source platform to programmatically author, schedule and monitor data pipelines and workflows defined as Python code.

Created at Airbnb and now a top-level Apache project, Airflow models each workflow as a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) of tasks written in Python, so pipelines become version-controlled, testable code instead of opaque cron jobs. It is a de facto standard for orchestrating ETL/ELT jobs, machine-learning pipelines and recurring automations across data warehouses, APIs and cloud services, with a rich web UI to visualise runs, inspect logs and retry failures. This deployment runs the CeleryExecutor stack (web server, scheduler, worker and triggerer) backed by PostgreSQL and Redis.

Key features
  • Workflows as Python code: DAGs with dependencies, branching, dynamic tasks and backfills
  • Rich web UI to trigger, monitor, inspect logs and retry runs with graph and Gantt views
  • Hundreds of provider packages: operators and hooks for databases, clouds, APIs and messaging
  • Distributed execution with a Celery worker pool plus a triggerer for deferrable async tasks
  • Flexible scheduling with cron, data intervals, sensors, SLAs, retries and alerting
  • REST API, pluggable executors, connections, variables and Fernet-encrypted secrets
Best for

Data and platform engineers who need to orchestrate reliable, observable pipelines on their own infrastructure. This is an advanced, resource-hungry stack (plan for several GB of RAM and adequate CPU) and is not meant for non-technical users, since DAGs execute arbitrary Python. On first boot an init container migrates the database and creates the admin user; sign in at the web UI with the admin username and the generated password, then drop your DAG files into the ./dags folder. Keep it behind HTTPS and protect your connection credentials.

x-airflow-common: &airflow-common image: apache/airflow:2.10.5 environment: &airflow-common-env AIRFLOW__CORE__EXECUTOR: CeleryExecutor AIRFLOW__DATABASE__SQL_ALCHEMY_CONN: postgresql+psycopg2://{{ postgres_user }}:${AIRFLOW_DB_PASSWORD}@postgres/{{ postgres_db }} AIRFLOW__CELERY__RESULT_BACKEND: db+postgresql://{{ postgres_user }}:${AIRFLOW_DB_PASSWORD}@postgres/{{ postgres_db }} AIRFLOW__CELERY__BROKER_URL: redis://redis:6379/0 AIRFLOW__CORE__FERNET_KEY: ${AIRFLOW_FERNET_KEY} AIRFLOW__WEBSERVER__SECRET_KEY: ${AIRFLOW_WEBSERVER_SECRET_KEY} AIRFLOW__CORE__DAGS_ARE_PAUSED_AT_CREATION: 'true' AIRFLOW__CORE__LOAD_EXAMPLES: '{{ load_examples }}' AIRFLOW__API__AUTH_BACKENDS: 'airflow.api.auth.backend.basic_auth,airflow.api.auth.backend.session' AIRFLOW__SCHEDULER__ENABLE_HEALTH_CHECK: 'true' AIRFLOW__WEBSERVER__EXPOSE_CONFIG: 'false' volumes: - ./dags:/opt/airflow/dags - ./logs:/opt/airflow/logs - ./plugins:/opt/airflow/plugins - ./config:/opt/airflow/config user: "{{ airflow_uid }}:0" depends_on: &airflow-common-depends-on postgres: condition: service_healthy redis: condition: service_healthy services: postgres: image: postgres:16 restart: unless-stopped environment: POSTGRES_USER: {{ postgres_user }} POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${AIRFLOW_DB_PASSWORD} POSTGRES_DB: {{ postgres_db }} volumes: - ./postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U {{ postgres_user }} -d {{ postgres_db }} interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 start_period: 20s expose: - 5432 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgres' networks: - internal redis: image: redis:7-alpine restart: unless-stopped healthcheck: test: - CMD - redis-cli - ping interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 start_period: 10s expose: - 6379 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_redis' networks: - internal airflow-init: <<: *airflow-common restart: on-failure entrypoint: /bin/bash command: - -c - | airflow db migrate airflow users create --role Admin --username "$${_AIRFLOW_WWW_USER_USERNAME}" --password "$${_AIRFLOW_WWW_USER_PASSWORD}" --firstname Admin --lastname User --email "$${_AIRFLOW_WWW_USER_USERNAME}@example.com" || true environment: <<: *airflow-common-env _AIRFLOW_WWW_USER_USERNAME: '{{ airflow_admin_username }}' _AIRFLOW_WWW_USER_PASSWORD: ${AIRFLOW_ADMIN_PASSWORD} user: "0:0" container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_init' networks: - internal airflow-webserver: <<: *airflow-common restart: unless-stopped command: webserver healthcheck: test: - CMD - curl - --fail - http://localhost:8080/health interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 5 start_period: 90s depends_on: <<: *airflow-common-depends-on airflow-init: condition: service_completed_successfully container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8080' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network airflow-scheduler: <<: *airflow-common restart: unless-stopped command: scheduler healthcheck: test: - CMD - curl - --fail - http://localhost:8974/health interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 5 start_period: 90s depends_on: <<: *airflow-common-depends-on airflow-init: condition: service_completed_successfully container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_scheduler' networks: - internal airflow-worker: <<: *airflow-common restart: unless-stopped command: celery worker healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - celery --app airflow.providers.celery.executors.celery_executor.app inspect ping -d "celery@$${HOSTNAME}" interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 5 start_period: 90s environment: <<: *airflow-common-env DUMB_INIT_SETSID: '0' depends_on: <<: *airflow-common-depends-on airflow-init: condition: service_completed_successfully container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_worker' networks: - internal airflow-triggerer: <<: *airflow-common restart: unless-stopped command: triggerer healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - airflow jobs check --job-type TriggererJob --hostname "$${HOSTNAME}" interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 5 start_period: 90s depends_on: <<: *airflow-common-depends-on airflow-init: condition: service_completed_successfully container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_triggerer' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
PostgreSQL password for the Airflow metadata database.
Fernet key used to encrypt connection passwords and variables at rest.
Flask secret key that signs the web UI session cookies.
Password for the initial Airflow admin (web UI) account.
PostgreSQL role that owns the Airflow metadata database.
Name of the Airflow metadata database.
Username for the initial Airflow admin (web UI) account.
Host UID that owns the mounted dags/logs/plugins/config folders.
Load the bundled example DAGs on first start.
